data1_prediction.disclaimer: This is a statistical project prediction for high-risk pregnancy screening, not a medical diagnosis. A qualified clinician must review any decision.
limitations: Predictions are statistical risk estimates, not medical diagnoses.
Data1 has unusually strong historical performance and should be treated cautiously because leakage or target-generation effects may be present.
Data1 and Data2 are not row-merged because they do not share patient or pregnancy identifiers.
Real-world performance depends on input data quality and population similarity.

data2_prediction.disclaimer: This is a statistical project prediction for high-risk pregnancy screening, not a medical diagnosis. A qualified clinician must review any decision.
limitations: Predictions are statistical risk estimates, not medical diagnoses.
Data1 is available as a separate dual-model artifact, but its unusually strong results may indicate leakage risk.
Dataset size and representativeness are limited.
Real-world performance depends on input data quality.
Data2 remains the backwards-compatible default model artifact for existing prediction workflows.

disclaimer: This is a statistical project prediction for high-risk pregnancy screening, not a medical diagnosis. A qualified clinician must review any decision.
scientific_framing: Data1 and Data2 are not row-merged because they do not share patient or pregnancy identifiers. Combined scoring is an inference-time weighted average of two separately trained model probabilities.
weight_rationale: Equal weights are used because Data1 and Data2 do not share pregnancy identifiers or a common validation cohort. Data1 performance is strong but scientifically suspicious, so it is not overweighted despite high holdout metrics.
